ddls:
CREATE TABLE "participants" (
  "participant_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each participant */,
  "participant_name" TEXT /* Name of the participant */,
  "group_id" INTEGER /* ID of the group to which the participant belongs */,
  "condition_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the participant's experimental condition */,
  "demographic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the participant's demographic information */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the participant was added to the study */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the participant's information was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("participant_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_participants_group_id FOREIGN KEY ("group_id") REFERENCES groups ("group_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "groups" (
  "group_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each group */,
  "group_name" TEXT /* Name of the group */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the group */,
  "condition_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the group's experimental condition */,
  "condition_description" TEXT /* Description of the experimental condition */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the group was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("group_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sets" (
  "set_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each set */,
  "set_number" INTEGER /* Number of the set (e.g., 1, 2, 3) */,
  "set_size" INTEGER /* Size of the set (number of items/questions in the set) */,
  "set_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the set type */,
  "set_type_description" TEXT /* Description of the set type */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the set was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("set_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "responses" (
  "response_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each response */,
  "participant_id" INTEGER /* ID of the participant who provided the response */,
  "set_id" INTEGER /* ID of the set to which the response belongs */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the response was recorded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("response_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_responses_participant_id FOREIGN KEY ("participant_id") REFERENCES participants ("participant_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_responses_set_id FOREIGN KEY ("set_id") REFERENCES sets ("set_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "item_responses" (
  "item_response_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each item response */,
  "response_id" INTEGER /* ID of the response to which the item response belongs */,
  "item_id" INTEGER /* ID of the item */,
  "response_value" INTEGER /* Value of the item response */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the item response was recorded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("item_response_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_item_responses_item_id FOREIGN KEY ("item_id") REFERENCES items ("item_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_item_responses_response_id FOREIGN KEY ("response_id") REFERENCES responses ("response_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "items" (
  "item_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each item */,
  "item_description" TEXT /* Description of the item */,
  "set_id" INTEGER /* ID of the set to which the item belongs */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the item was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("item_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_items_set_id FOREIGN KEY ("set_id") REFERENCES sets ("set_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "permission_description" TEXT /* Description of the permission */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the role was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role assigned to the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user was added to the system */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user's information was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_permissions" (
  "user_permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user permission */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user permission was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_permissions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_permissions_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_id")
)
